channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Death Stranding 2: On the Beach details new features due out alongside PC version
item.content:encoded:
Get the details below.

■ About the Game

For PC players just learning about Death Stranding 2: On the Beach, the game is a standalone sequel to Death Stranding, in which you step into the boots of Sam as he sets out on a brand-new adventure to connect both Mexico and Australia to the Chiral network and ultimately save humanity from extinction once again.

A Hideo Kojima game,Death Stranding 2: On the Beach stars Norman Reedus, Léa Seydoux, Elle Fanning, Shioli Kutsuna, Troy Baker, Luca Marinelli, George Miller, Fatih Akin, Nicolas Winding Refn, Guillermo del Toro, Tommie Earl Jenkins, and Alissa Jung.

■ New Features

Get Wilder with new “to the wilder” Mode and More

A brand-new challenge is coming to the PC version of Death Stranding 2: On the Beach. For those seeking the ultimate gameplay challenge to accompany the story, “to the wilder” mode tests your porter skills to the extreme in merciless environments filled with deadly enemies, and completing orders will require every ounce of ingenuity you can muster. Although there is no turning back once you embark upon this punishing journey, your reward will be a sense of satisfaction that is truly priceless, and you can expect genuine gratitude from the other porters you help.

New Fieldware and VR Training Area

Sam can also access new fieldware including new bandanas which can be unlocked through the newly added “Trapped in a Strange Realm”; a VR training area where you can relive Sam’s epic confrontations with Neil.

Chiral Feline Visits

Porters can also expect to see visits from the Chiral Feline in the private room aboard the DHV Magellan, new additions—including the Chiral Feline—to Photo Mode.

New Features Also Coming to PlayStation 5

We’re happy to confirm that all these content additions will be coming to the PlayStation 5 version of the game at the same time.

■ PC Version Technical Features

Thanks to our friends at Nixxes Software, features exclusive to the PC version include full integration with NVIDIA’s DLSS, AMD’s FSR, and Intel’s XeSS and upscaling and frame generation technologies as well as uncapped frame rates and customized graphical settings, super-ultrawide (32:9) support for gameplay, with standard ultrawide (21:9) support for in-game cut scenes, full mouse and keyboard support with custom keybind mapping, full DualSense controller integration, with haptic feedback, adaptive trigger functionality, and Spatial Sound Support. Standard ultrawide (21:9) support will also be made available for the PlayStation 5 version.

Additionally, players on PC with high-end PC hardware will have the additional option to enable Ray Tracing on Reflections and Ambient Occlusion via additional settings. When enabled, ray-traced reflections are applied on surfaces such as water and tar, while ray-traced ambient occlusion applies ambient lighting affects to scenes, resulting in more realistic shadows in crevices, corners, and between objects. These additional PC options are aimed at players with powerful hardware that want to push visual fidelity beyond the “Very High” graphics settings the game already offers. Though would like to note that Ray Tracing is not part of the minimum or recommended specifications for the core experience and is not required to enjoy the game as intended.

Death Stranding 2: On the Beach is available now for PlayStation 5.

Watch a new trailer below.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/death-stranding-2-on-the-beach-details-new-features-due-out-alongside-pc-version
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Demeo x Dungeons & Dragons: Battlemarked ‘Warlock’ update now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS VR2
  • PS5
  • Quest

New hero, content, and expanded language support.

Resolution Games has released the “Warlock” update for cooperative tactical adventure game Demeo x Dungeons & Dragons: Battlemarked, which adds the Warlock hero, a new One Shot experience, additional language support, and the removal of hirelings.

“With this update, we’re expanding what it means to gather your party in Battlemarked,” said Resolution Games CEO Tommy Palm in a press release. “The Warlock introduces a class built around tension, temptation and tactical risk while our expanded language support ensures that more players around the world can experience these great adventures together. This is about deepening the fantasy, strengthening player agency, and continuing to build Battlemarked into a living world that grows alongside its community.”

Here is an overview of the update, via Resolution Games:

  • A New Hero Emerges Among a Frozen Side Quest – With this update, Azruss Drevontir joins Battlemarked as a fully playable Warlock hero, wielding eldritch magic born of infernal promise. His class-based deck unleashes devastating pact-bound spells, battlefield manipulation, and volatile power that rewards bold strategy and precise coordination. Complete this side quest, and the Warlock becomes permanently unlocked—ready to join your party immediately in future campaigns and adventures.
  • “Auril’s Cradle” One Shot – From the depths of winter’s cruelty and adding to the adventure, this update also introduces “Auril’s Cradle” as a new One Shot experience. Now available as a standalone One Shot for players who have unlocked Ythryn in the second campaign, this adventure offers a punishing test of tactical mastery. Designed to stand among the more formidable trials in Battlemarked, “Auril’s Cradle” launches without selectable difficulty modes—though additional difficulty options will arrive in a future update. Yet even without scaling modifiers, the shifting threats and strategic demands of this One Shot offer immense replayability. Each descent into the frostbound crucible presents new risks, new decisions, and new opportunities for glory—or defeat. For new players—or veterans replaying the second campaign—clearing “Auril’s Cradle” within the campaign will unlock the One Shot version for future standalone play.
  • The Realms Expand: New Language Support – The call to adventure now echoes farther than ever. In today’s update, Demeo x Dungeons & Dragons: Battlemarked now supports Italian, Brazilian Portuguese, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional). These additions make it easier than ever for friends around the world to gather their party, strategize, and step into the Dungeons & Dragons universe together.
  • Phase 2: Hirelings Redesign – Following on to the two-phase hirelings redesign announcement as a result of player feedback, today’s update marks Phase 2 of Battlemarked‘s hero system redesign, delivering one of the most significant gameplay evolutions yet: hirelings have been fully removed from the game. Moving forward, the only heroes in play are heroes created by players themselves. This shift ensures that every hero at the table is player-driven, reinforcing Battlemarked‘s focus on co-operative strategy, meaningful decision-making, and the shared thrill of overcoming impossible odds together.

Demeo x Dungeons & Dragons: Battlemarked is available now for PlayStation 5, PlayStation VR2, PC and SteamVR, and Quest.

Watch a new trailer below.

“Warlock” Update Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/demeo-x-dungeons-dragons-battlemarked-warlock-update-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Game Pass adds Disco Elysium: The Final Cut, Resident Evil 7: Biohazard, Final Fantasy IV, and more in late March
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Smartphone
  • Xbox One
  • Xbox Series

Peppa Pig World Adventures and Mad Streets leaving soon.

Microsoft has announced new titles coming to Game Pass in late March, including Disco Elysium: The Final Cut, Resident Evil 7: Biohazard, Final Fantasy IV, and more.

Get the details below.

■ Available Today

  • DreamWorks Gabby’s Dollhouse: Ready to Party (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – DreamWorks Gabby’s Dollhouse: Ready to Party invites players into a colorful, music-filled adventure with Gabby and her friends. Explore iconic rooms, enjoy playful mini-games, and celebrate creativity and teamwork in a family-friendly party experience.

■ Coming Soon

March 18

  • South of Midnight (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) Now with Game Pass Premium; joining Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass – South of Midnight is an action adventure from Compulsion Games. Explore the mythos and confront mysterious creatures of the Deep South in this modern folktale while learning to weave an ancient power to surmount obstacles and face the pain haunting your hometown.
  • The Alters (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) Now with Game Pass Premium; joining Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass – The Alters is a science-fiction survival game where you play as Jan, stranded on a hostile planet. Create alternate versions of yourself, each shaped by different life choices, to survive, gather resources, and navigate deep moral dilemmas. Your past becomes your crew in this unique science-fiction survival.

March 19

  • Disco Elysium: The Final Cut (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – Disco Elysium: The Final Cut is a groundbreaking role-playing game. You’re a detective with a unique skill system at your disposal and a whole city to carve your path across. Interrogate unforgettable characters, crack murders or take bribes. Become a hero or an absolute disaster of a human being.

March 24

  • Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – Two larger-than-life heroes, Ichiban Kasuga and Kazuma Kiryu, are brought together by the hand of fate… or perhaps something more sinister. Live it up in Japan and explore all that Hawaii has to offer in an RPG adventure so big it spans the Pacific. Experience one-of-a-kind combat with dynamic, fast-paced RPG battles where the battlefield becomes your weapon, and anything goes.

March 25

  • Absolum (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – Absolum combines top-of-the-class beat ’em up action with modern roguelite elements in a beautifully handcrafted setting. Embark on a perilous quest to free the world of Talamh from the tyranny of Sun King Azra and experience all the vibes of the fantasy arcade hits of the past.

March 26

  • Nova Roma (Game Preview) (PC) Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass – Available Day One with Xbox Game Pass! As the once-great Roman Empire falls into decadence and decay, you lead citizens in search of new lands hoping to build Nova Roma. Starting from a humble village, you must appease the gods with grand temples, sate your citizens’ needs, and build a society that will be the envy of the world.

March 30

  • The Long Dark (Cloud, Console, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – The Long Dark is a thoughtful, exploration-survival experience that challenges solo players to think for themselves as they explore an expansive frozen wilderness in the aftermath of a geomagnetic disaster. There are no zombies—only you, the cold, and all the threats Mother Nature can muster.

March 31

  • Resident Evil 7: Biohazard (Cloud, Console, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – Resident Evil 7: Biohazard marks a bold new chapter for the Resident Evil series. Set in rural America, players experience intense first-person survival horror, blending tense exploration, a terrifying atmosphere, and close-up encounters that return the franchise to its roots.

April 2

  • Barbie Horse Trails (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – Adventure awaits in Barbie Horse Trails, an open-world experience set in scenic Canterbury Trails Park. Gallop across wildflower fields, snap photos, dig up treasures, unlock collectibles, and help bring new life to the park alongside your trusted horse, Lucky.
  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC) Now with Game Pass Premium; joining Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass – Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 combines stylish turn-based strategy with real-time action in a Belle Époque-inspired French fantasy world. After winning the 2025 Game of the Year, the adventure expanded with the whimsical Verso’s Drafts update, offering new challenges and fan-requested features to this unforgettable journey.

April 7

  • Final Fantasy IV (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – Dive into a classic RPG with Final Fantasy IV. The Kingdom of Baron begins to attack the surrounding countries. Cecil the dark knight joins forces with Kain the dragoon, Rosa the white mage, Rydia the summoner, and more to fight against Baron.

■ In Case You Missed It

March 3

  • Minishoot’ Adventures (Console, Cloud, Handheld, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – Fly into a charming, handcrafted world and live an adventure that mixes up open exploration with crispy twin-stick shooter action. Fight your way from the shiny overworld to the deepest caves, improve your ship and overcome the dungeons’ bosses to rescue your friends!

■ Game Updates

March 18

  • Valorant (PC and Console) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Essential, PC Game Pass – Valorant is releasing a new Agent! Game Pass subscribers now have access to all agents with Game Pass Benefits. Valorant‘s new Croatian Controller Miks amplifies the vibes, equalizes the playing field, and always keeps your team on the right tempo using a kit of team-enhancing and enemy-disrupting abilities.

March 19

  • Sea of Thieves Season 19 (Console and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass – Season 19 of Sea of Thieves unfolds across three Acts, introducing improved Hourglass player-versus-player battles, new limited-time events for earning and spending Doubloons, and large-scale multi-ship faction clashes, alongside fresh cosmetics, achievements, and 100 levels of Seasonal Renown.

■ In-Game Benefits

Available Now

  • Tom Clancy’s The Division 2 (Console) – Battleworn Secret Service Pack: Gear up and stand your ground with the Battleworn Secret Service Pack! This pack includes one Secret Service Outfit, one rare quality emote, and one exotic M4A1 weapon skin.
  • Skate. (Console, Game Pass Ultimate only) – Supercharge Pack: Drop in to San Vansterdam with 30 new items in the Supercharge Pack and show off your skate style. This pack includes three Decks, two sets of Trucks, two sets of Wheels, eight Stickers, seven Icons, and eight Titles.

■ Leaving March 31

The following games are leaving the Game Pass library soon. Jump back in to tie up any loose ends or save up to 20 percent off your purchase to keep the fun going!

  • Peppa Pig World Adventures (Cloud, Console, PC)
  • Mad Streets (Cloud, Console, PC)


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/game-pass-adds-disco-elysium-the-final-cut-resident-evil-7-biohazard-final-fantasy-iv-and-more-in-late-march
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
